volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Eunuch (Section 3.2.493b - Section 3.5.576b)

sections.label: Section 3.2.493b
sections.translation: It is not a proper thing for a general to be walking in the street with a mistress .

sections.label: Section 3.2.495
sections.original: Quid tibi ego multa dicam ? domini similis es .
sections.translation: Why should I use many words with you ? You are the very ape of your master .

sections.label: Section 3.2.496
sections.translation: Ha , ha , ha !

sections.label: Section 3.2.496b
sections.translation: What are you laughing at ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.496c
sections.translation: At what you were mentioning just now ; that saying , too , about the Rhodian , recurred to my mind . But Thais is coming out .

sections.label: Section 3.2.498b
sections.original: Abi prae , curre , ut sint domi
sections.translation: You go before ; take care that every thing is ready at home .

sections.label: Section 3.2.499b
sections.translation: Very well .

sections.label: Section 3.2.499c
sections.translation: Take care , Pythias ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.500
sections.original: Fac cures , si Chremes huc forte advenerit ,
sections.translation: and be sure that if Chremes should happen to come , [*] to beg him to wait ; if that is not convenient , then to come again ; if he can not do that , bring him to me .

sections.label: Section 3.2.503
sections.translation: I ’ ll do so .

sections.label: Section 3.2.503b
sections.translation: Well , what else was I intending to say ? O , do you take particular care of that young woman ;

sections.label: Section 3.2.505
sections.translation: be sure that you keep at home .

sections.label: Section 3.2.505b
sections.translation: Let us begone .

sections.label: Section 3.2.505c
sections.translation: You follow me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.506
sections.translation: Why , really , the more and more I think of it , I shouldn ’ t be surprised if this Thais should be doing me some great mischief ; so cunningly do I perceive myself beset by her . Even on the occasion when she first requested me to be fetched to her

sections.label: Section 3.3.510
sections.original: Roget quis Quid tibi cum illa ? ne noram quidem ;
sections.translation: ( any one might ask me , What business had you with her ? Really I don ’ t know .) When I came , she found an excuse for me to remain there ; she said that she had been offering a sacrifice , [*] and that she was desirous to speak upon some important business with me . Even then I had a suspicion that all these things were being done for her artful purposes . She takes her place beside

sections.label: Section 3.3.515
sections.original: Mecum , mihi sese dare , sermonem quaerere .
sections.translation: me ; pays every attention to me ; seeks and opportunity of conversation . When the conversation flagged , she turned off to this point — how long since my father and mother died ? I said that it was now a long time ago . Whether I had any country-house at Sunium , and how far from the sea ? I suppose that this has taken her fancy , and she expects to get it away from me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.520
sections.original: Postremo , ecqua inde parva periisset soror ?
sections.translation: Then at last , whether any little sister of mine had been lost from there ; whether any person was with her ; what she had about her when she was lost ; whether any one could recognize her . Why should she make these inquiries ? Unless , perhaps , she pretends — so great is her assurance — that she herself is the same person that was formerly lost when a little girl .

sections.label: Section 3.3.525
sections.original: Verum ea si vivit annos nata est sedecim ,
sections.translation: But if she is alive , she is sixteen years old , not older ; whereas Thais is somewhat older than I am . She has sent to press me earnestly to come . Either let her speak out what she wants , or not be troublesome ; I assuredly shall not come a third time ( knocking at the door of THAIS ) . Ho ! there , ho ! there ! Is any one here ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.530
sections.translation: It ’ s I , Chremes .

sections.label: Section 3.3.530b
sections.translation: O most charming , dear creature !

sections.label: Section 3.3.531
sections.translation: I said there was a design upon me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.531b
sections.translation: Thais entreated you most earnestly to come again to-morrow .

sections.label: Section 3.3.532b
sections.translation: I ’ m going into the country .

sections.label: Section 3.3.533b
sections.translation: Do , there ’ s a dear sir .

sections.label: Section 3.3.533c
sections.translation: I can not , I tell you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.533d
sections.original: At tu apud nos hic mane ,
sections.translation: Then stay here at our house till she comes back .

sections.label: Section 3.3.534b
sections.translation: Nothing less likely .

sections.label: Section 3.3.534c
sections.translation: Why , my dear Chremes ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.535
sections.translation: Away to perdition with you !

sections.label: Section 3.3.535b
sections.original: Si istuc ita certum est tibi ,
sections.translation: If you are so determined about it , pray do step over to the place where she is .

sections.label: Section 3.3.536b
sections.translation: I ’ ll go there .

sections.label: Section 3.3.537
sections.original: Abi Dorias cito hunc deduce ad militem .
sections.translation: Here , Dorias ( DORIAS enters ) , show this person directly to the Captain ’ s .

sections.label: Section 3.4.538
sections.original: Heri aliquot adolescentuli coimus in Piraeeo ,
sections.translation: Yesterday some young fellows of us agreed together at the Piraeus that we were to go shares today in a club-entertainment . We gave Chaerea charge of this matter ;

sections.label: Section 3.4.540
sections.original: Praefecimus ; dati annuli ; locus , tempus constitutum est .
sections.translation: our rings were given [*] as pledges ; the place and time arranged . The time has now gone by ; at the place appointed there was nothing ready . The fellow himself is nowhere to be met with ; I neither know what to say nor what to suppose . Now the rest have commissioned me with his business , to look for him . I ’ ll go see , therefore , if he ’ s at home . But who ’ s this , I wonder , coming out of Thais ’ s ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.545
sections.original: Is est , an non est ? ipsus est . Quid hoc hominis ? qui hic ornatus est ?
sections.translation: Is it he , or is it not ? ’ Tis the very man ! What sort of being is this ? What kind of garb is this ? What mischief is going on now ? I can not sufficiently wonder or conjecture . But , whatever it is , I should like first at a distance to try and find out .

sections.label: Section 3.5.548
sections.original: Numquis hic est ? nemo est : numquis hinc me sequitur ? nemo homo est .
sections.translation: Is there any body here ? There ’ s no one . Is there any one following me from there ? There ’ s not a person . Now am I not at liberty to give vent to these raptures ? O supreme Jupiter !

sections.label: Section 3.5.550
sections.original: Nunc est profecto interfici cum perpeti me possum ,
sections.translation: now assuredly is the time for me to meet my death , [*] when I can so well endure it ; lest my life should sully this ecstasy with some disaster . But is there now no inquisitive person to be intruding upon me , to be following me wherever I do , to be deafening me , worrying me to death , with asking questions ; why thus transported , or why so overjoyed , whither I ’ m going , whence I ’ m come , where

sections.label: Section 3.5.555
sections.original: Testitum hunc nactus ; quid mihi quaeram ; sanus sim , anne insaniam ?
sections.translation: I got this garb , what is my object , whether I ’ m in my senses or whether downright mad ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.556
sections.original: Adibo , atque ab eo gratiam hanc quam video velle inibo .
sections.translation: I ’ ll accost him , and I ’ ll do him the favor which I see he ’ s wishing for . ( Accosting him .) Chaerea , why are you thus transported ? What ’ s the object of this garb ? Why is it that you ’ re so overjoyed ? What is the meaning of this ? Are you quite right in your senses ? Why do you stare at me ? What have you to say ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.559b
sections.original: O festus dies hominis ! amice salve .
sections.translation: O joyous day ! O welcome , my friend !

sections.label: Section 3.5.560
sections.original: Nemo est hominum , quem ego nunc magis cuperem videre quam te .
sections.translation: There ’ s not one in all the world whom I would rather wish to see at this moment that yourself .

sections.label: Section 3.5.561
sections.translation: Pray , do tell me what all this means .

sections.label: Section 3.5.561b
sections.original: Immo ego te obsecro hercle ut audias .
sections.translation: Nay rather , i ’ faith , I beg of you to listen to me . Do you know the mistress whom my brother is so fond of ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.562b
sections.translation: I know her ; I suppose you mean Thais ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.563
sections.translation: The very same .

sections.label: Section 3.5.563b
sections.translation: So far I recollect .

sections.label: Section 3.5.563c
sections.original: Quaedam hodie est ei dono data
sections.translation: To-day a certain damsel was presented to her . Why now should I extol or commend her beauty to you , Antipho ,

sections.label: Section 3.5.565
sections.original: Cum ipsum me noris quam elegans formarum spectator siem ?
sections.translation: since you yourself know how nice a judge of beauty I am ? I have been smitten by her .

sections.label: Section 3.5.566b
sections.translation: Do you say so ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.566c
sections.translation: If you saw her , I am sure you would say she ’ s exquisite . What need of many words ? I fell in love with her . By good luck there was at our house a certain Eunuch , whom my brother had purchased for Thais , and he had not as yet been sent to her . On this occasion , Parmeno ,

sections.label: Section 3.5.570
sections.translation: our servant , made a suggestion to me , which I adopted .

sections.label: Section 3.5.570b
sections.translation: What was it ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.570c
sections.translation: Be quiet , and you shall hear the sooner ; to change clothes with him , and order myself to be taken there in his stead .

sections.label: Section 3.5.572
sections.translation: What , instead of the Eunuch ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.572b
sections.translation: The fact .

sections.label: Section 3.5.572c
sections.original: Quid ex ea re tandem ut caperes commodi ?
sections.translation: To receive what advantage , pray , from this plan ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.573
sections.original: Rogas ? viderem , audirem , essem una quacum cupiebam Antipho .
sections.translation: Do you ask ? That I might see , hear , and be in company with her whom I loved , Antipho . Is that a slight motive , or a poor reason ? I was presented to the woman .

sections.label: Section 3.5.575
sections.original: Illa ilico ubi me accepit laeta vero ad se abducit domum :
sections.translation: She , as soon as she received me , joyfully took me home to her house and intrusted the damsel —

sections.label: Section 3.5.576b
sections.translation: To whom ? To you ?
